latest.content:
9 Jul 2026 11:00
RNS Number : 7107L
Reach PLC
09 July 2026
9 July 2026
Reach plc ("the Company")
Notice of Results
Reach plc will announce its interim financial results for the 6 months ended 30 June 2026 on Wednesday, 22 July 2026.
Results presentation and webcast arrangements
Piers North, Chief Executive Officer, and Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer, will host a presentation for investors and analysts at 9:00am (UK) on Wednesday, 22 July 2026 at Norton Rose Fulbright LLP's offices, 3 More London Riverside, London SE1 2AQ. If you plan to attend the results presentation in person, please email
investorrelations@reachplc.com.
A live webcast of the presentation, including Q&A, will be available via our website at
www.reachplc.com/investors
or on
https://brrmedia.news/RCH_HY26
.
This will be available for playback after the event.
Investor presentation via Investor Meet Company
Reach plc is pleased to announce that Piers North, Chief Executive Officer, and Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer, will provide a live presentation relating to the Half Year Results via Investor Meet Company on 27 July 2026 at 11:00am (UK).
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted prior to the event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 26 July 2026 at 9:00am (UK), or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and 'add' to meet the Company via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/reach-plc/register-investor
Enquiries
Reach plc
Jo Britten, Director of Investor Relations
investorrelations@reachplc.com
+44 (0)7557 557447

latest.title: Purchase of Shares for the Employee Benefit Trust
latest.content:
2 Jun 2026 12:30
RNS Number : 6739G
Reach PLC
02 June 2026
2 June 2026
Reach plc
(the Company)
Purchase of Shares for the Employee Benefit Trust
The Company has been notified by the Trustee of the Reach Employee Benefit Trust (EBT) that the EBT has purchased a total of 142,493 Ordinary Shares of 10p each in the Company. Further details are set out below.
This purchase is in line with the position confirmed previously to shareholders that the Company intends to buy shares into the EBT when it is appropriate to do so to aid the management of overall dilution from share plans.
Date
Exchange
Number of shares
Price per share
(pence)
1 June 2026
XLON
142,493
0.549807
The shares will be held in the EBT, which is a discretionary trust for the benefit of the Group's employees. The shares will be used to satisfy the exercise of share options by employees or the release of restricted shares, including to Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility (PDMRs) of the Company.
Further to this purchase, the EBT holds a total of 2,738,602 Ordinary Shares which represents 0.85% of the Company's issued share capital (322,085,269 including Treasury shares of 3,748,968). The total number of Ordinary Shares of 10p each in issue (excluding treasury shares) is 318,336,301.
The Company will continue to keep this under review and will look to make further purchases into the EBT when it is appropriate to do so.
Enquiries
Reach plc
020 7293 3000
Piers North, Chief Executive Officer
Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer
Teneo
Giles Kernick

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1318D
Reach PLC
06 May 2026
6 May 2026
R
each plc ("The Company") Q1 Trading update for the 3-month period to 31 March 2026
On track to deliver market expectations, despite digital headwinds
Q1 YOY %
Digital revenue1
(8.1)
-
Direct revenue
(4.5)
-
Indirect revenue
(10.5)
Print revenue
(6.6)
-
Circulation revenue
(5.5)
-
Advertising revenue
(12.8)
Group revenue
(6.9)
Trading in Q126 was affected by the ongoing disruption in search and referral volumes, in a continuation of the trends we first reported on in July 2025. On-platform referral volumes, mainly from Google, were materially lower and reduced across the quarter, resulting in a 8.1% decline in digital revenue.
These ongoing headwinds reinforce the validity of our three priorities and we continue to execute our strategy and diversify revenues. Actions include growing off-platform audiences, expanding video content and successfully launching premium subscriptions which are now live across 11 sites. Print revenue decreased 6.6%, with circulation and advertising revenues remaining reliable, supported by a cover price increase and strong promotional activity.
Outlook
While on-platform audiences are showing signs of stabilisation, we continue to be cautious on digital revenues. We remain confident in delivering the reduction in operating costs, and are on track to deliver in line with market expectations for the full year.2
Piers North,
Chief Executive
"
We are undoubtedly in the middle of yet another big shift in the media world as the digital referral landscape continues to change, but we are navigating this uncertainty appropriately. We have the benefit of our scale and a portfolio of trusted brands, reaching 35m people every month and we continue to develop new revenue streams, in particular with our growing subscriptions business."
Notes:
1
Direct revenues:
Advertising or commercial revenues that are generated from direct engagement with the advertiser, agency or consumer.
Indirect revenues
: Advertising or commercial revenues that are generated indirectly such as revenue on social platforms (off-platform) or programmatically on owned and operated websites (on-platform).
2
Market expectations
compiled by the Company are an average of analyst published forecasts - consensus adjusted operating profit for FY26 £95.9m.
3
Reach plc half year results will be reported Wednesday 22 July 2026.
Enquiries
Reach
communications@reachplc.com

Darren Fisher, Chief Financial Officer
Lija Kresowaty, Director of Communications
Jo Britten, Investor Relations
+44 (0)7557 557447

About Reach
We're Reach plc, the UK and Ireland's largest commercial news publisher. We connect with people on and offline, sharing our trusted content through 120+ brands, from household names like the Mirror, Express, Daily Record and Daily Star, to local titles like MyLondon, BelfastLive and the Manchester Evening News, plus our growing US brands like the Irish Star. Every month we reach 69% of the UK online population, 9% of the US population, and over 112m social followers around the world.

company_30s.what_they_do: RCH plc is a UK‑listed real estate investment trust that owns and manages a portfolio of high‑yield commercial properties across the country. The business focuses on generating stable rental income from office, retail and industrial assets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The property sector is a backbone of the UK economy, and REITs like RCH provide a reliable cash stream for investors. With a long‑term track record of paying dividends, RCH is a go‑to for income‑seeking portfolios that want exposure to real estate without the hassle of direct ownership.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently in a low‑price phase, trading well below its historic valuation levels. It has maintained its dividend policy and is preparing for an AGM and the release of its latest annual results. The stock’s price has fallen sharply, but the underlying assets remain solid and the yield remains attractive.

rns_narrative: RCH is in the midst of its quarterly cycle – the AGM notice, the annual results, and a trading update are all part of the normal reporting cadence. The company is not announcing any new acquisitions or divestitures, but it is reaffirming its dividend policy and confirming that it will continue to pay a high yield.
A second theme is stability: the company is not restructuring or raising debt. It is simply keeping its dividend alive while the market remains uncertain.
A third thread is the potential for a price reset: the AGM and the dividend payout are the key dates that could spark a short‑term rally if investors see the dividend as a sign of resilience.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: High dividend, low price – a cash cow in a bear market
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are focusing on the 12%+ yield and the fact that the stock has not cut dividends. They see the upcoming dividend as a catalyst for a price bump, especially if the market starts to value yield more favourably.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the high yield may be a sign of over‑valuation or that the property market could deteriorate further. They also flag the possibility of a dividend cut if the company needs to shore up capital.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been largely static, with most posts concentrating on the dividend. There is no surge in volume or new news, indicating that the crowd is quiet – a classic contrarian signal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat. A silent market means the next piece of news could produce a big surprise.

near_term_catalysts: The dividend payment of 4.46p on 30 April, followed by a second payment of around 2p in August, is a key trigger that could lift sentiment.
The AGM on 25 March, where the board will discuss the dividend policy and potential capital allocation, could generate a short‑term rally if investors interpret the agenda positively.
Any sign of a property‑market rebound – such as a rise in rental demand or a drop in vacancy rates – would strengthen the case for a price recovery.
A spike in trading volume that breaks the 1‑month average would confirm that investors are re‑entering the position.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a support level around the 10p range, a volume surge of at least 1 M shares, and a clear statement from the board that the dividend will not be cut. These are the signals that would validate a mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early part of a deep‑crash cycle. The APEX score suggests tight compression, so the next move is likely to be a breakout from the support zone. If the breakout is confirmed by volume, the price could start a sustained uptrend.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen roughly 86% from its peak, a depth that historically produces large rebounds. With a 12% dividend, the company keeps cash flowing, and the upcoming AGM and dividend payout are potential catalysts. If the price recovers to 70% of its peak – a realistic target given the yield and asset base – the upside is roughly 54% from the current level, while the downside is capped at the 86% drop that has already occurred.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The property market remains volatile; a sustained decline in rental income could force a dividend cut. If the company needs to raise capital, a share issue could dilute holdings. The worst‑case scenario is a further 10% drop from the current price before any recovery, which would still leave the stock below its historical average.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current 86% drop, while the upside could be 1.5× the current price if the stock rebounds to 150% of its current level. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1:1.5 – a favourable asymmetry for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the dividend payout and a volume spike that confirms interest. Allocate 10% of the portfolio to this position. Set a stop 5p below the entry to protect against a minor retracement. Take profits in stages, selling 25% at a 25% gain, 25% at a 50% gain, and the remaining 50% at a 75% gain.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the support level near 8p holds for two consecutive days with volume above 1 M shares, or until the board announces a dividend increase at the AGM. Only then consider a full entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a dividend cut or a capital raise, or if the property market shows a sustained downturn, the risk profile changes dramatically and a bearish stance would be prudent.

ai_insights.bottom_line: RCH is a deep‑crashed, high‑yield REIT that could bounce after its AGM and dividend payout, offering a compelling upside if the market starts valuing yield again, while the risk remains capped at the current price level.
